title: Angles in circles
description: Solve problems about angles as part of a circle.
topic_path: Module 4: Angle measure and plane figures

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the circle below. $\angle{NOP}$ is $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of a full turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**How many degrees does $\angle{NOP}$ measure?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

$\angle{NOP}$ is equal to $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

$\dfrac{1}{2}$ of a turn is equal to $180\degree$. 

[[Ōśā image 1]]
$\angle{NOP}$ measure is $180$ degrees.

options.alt: A circle with center point O and two rays. Ray ON points to 9 o'clock. Ray OP points to 3 oŌĆÖclock.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the circle below. $\angle{ABC}$ is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of a full turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**How many degrees does $\angle{ABC}$ measure?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

$\angle{ABC}$ is equal to $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

$\dfrac{1}{3}$ of a turn is equal to $120\degree$. 

[[Ōśā image 1]]
$\angle{ABC}$ is $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of a full turn, so its measure is $120$ degrees.

options.alt: A circle with center point B and two rays. Ray BC points to 5 o'clock. Ray BA points to 9 oŌĆÖclock.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the circle below. $\angle{XYZ}$ is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of a full turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**How many degrees does $\angle{XYZ}$ measure?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

$\angle{XYZ}$ is equal to $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

$\dfrac{1}{4}$ of a turn is equal to $90\degree$. 

[[Ōśā image 1]]
$\angle{XYZ}$ is $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of a full turn, so its measure is $90$ degrees.

options.alt: A circle with center point Y and two rays. Ray YX points to 9 o'clock. Ray YZ points to 6 o'clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the circle below. $\angle{RST}$ is $\dfrac{1}{6}$ of a full turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**How many degrees does $\angle{RST}$ measure?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

$\angle{RST}$ is equal to $\dfrac{1}{6}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

$\dfrac{1}{6}$ of a turn is equal to $60\degree$. 


[[Ōśā image 1]]
$\angle{RST}$ is $\dfrac{1}{6}$ of a full turn, so its measure is $60$ degrees.

options.alt: A circle divided into four equal parts with angle RST comprising about two-thirds of a section.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the circle below. $\angle{DEF}$ is $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of a full turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**How many degrees does $\angle{DEF}$ measure?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

$\angle{DEF}$ is equal to $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

$\dfrac{1}{8}$ of a turn is equal to $45\degree$. 

[[Ōśā image 1]]
$\angle{DEF}$ is $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of a full turn, so its measure is $45$ degrees.

options.alt: A circle with center point E and two rays. Ray EF points to between 11 and 12 o'clock, slightly closer to 11 o'clock. Ray ED points to between 12 and 1 o'clock, slightly closer to 1 o'clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the circle below. $\angle{ABC}$ is $\dfrac{1}{12}$ of a full turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**How many degrees does $\angle{ABC}$ measure?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

$\angle{ABC}$ is equal to $\dfrac{1}{12}$ of a turn.


[[Ōśā image 1]]

$\dfrac{1}{12}$ of a turn is equal to $30\degree$. 


[[Ōśā image 1]]
$\angle{ABC}$ is $\dfrac{1}{12}$ of a full turn, so its measure is $30$ degrees.

options.alt: A circle with center point B and two rays. Ray BC points to 9 o'clock. Ray BA points to 10 oŌĆÖclock.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many $30\degree$ angles does it take to make a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$.

How many $30$'s are in $360$?

$360 \div 30= 12$


[[Ōśā image 1]]
It takes twelve $30\degree$ angles to make a full turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many $45\degree$ angles does it take to make a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$.

How many $45$'s are in $360$?

$360 \div 8= 45$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
It takes eight $45\degree$ angles to make a full turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many $60\degree$ angles does it take to make a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$.

How many $60$'s are in $360$?

$360 \div 6 = 60$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
It takes six $60\degree$ angles to make a full turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many $90\degree$ angles does it take to make a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$.

How many $90$'s are in $360$?

$360 \div 4 = 90$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
It takes four $90\degree$ angles to make a full turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many $120\degree$ angles does it take to make a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$.

How many $120$'s are in $360$?

$360 \div 3= 120$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
It takes three $120\degree$ angles to make a full turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many $180\degree$ angles does it take to make a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$.

How many $180$'s are in $360$?

$360 \div 2= 180$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
It takes two $180\degree$ angles to make a full turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many $360\degree$ angles does it take to make a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$.

How many $360$'s are in $360$?

$360 \div 1 = 360$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
It takes one $360\degree$ angle to make a full turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many degrees are in $\dfrac12$ of a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

To find a *half* of something, we divide by $2$.

$360 \div 2 = 180$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
There are $180\degree$ in a half of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many degrees are in  $\dfrac13$ of a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

To find a *third* of something, we divide by $3$.

$360 \div 3 = 120$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
There are $120\degree$ in a third of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many degrees are in a quarter of a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

To find a *quarter* of something, we divide by $4$.

$360 \div 4 = 90$

[[Ōśā image 1]]
There are $90\degree$ in a quarter of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many degrees are in  $\dfrac16$ of a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

To find a *sixth* of something, we divide by $6$.

$360 \div 6 = 60$


[[Ōśā image 1]]
There are $60\degree$ in a sixth of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many degrees are in  $\dfrac18$ of a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

To find a *eighth* of something, we divide by $8$.

$360 \div 8 = 45$


[[Ōśā image 1]]
There are $45\degree$ in an eighth of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many degrees are in $\dfrac1{12}$ of a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

To find a *twelfth* of something, we divide by $12$.

$360 \div 12 = 30$


[[Ōśā image 1]]
There are $30\degree$ in a twelfth of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many degrees are in $\dfrac34$ of a full turn?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints: A full turn is $360\degree$.

To find a *fourth* of something, we divide by $4$.

$360 \div 4 = 90$

[[Ōśā image 1]]

So, $\dfrac34$ of something is equal to $90\degree$ three times.

$90\times3=270$
There are $270\degree$ in $\dfrac34$ of a turn.

assessment_items.question_content:
The circle is divided into $360$ equal parts. Point $X$ represents the center of the circle.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**Which angle measures $45\degree$?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$ around the center.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

How much of a turn is $45\degree$?

$45\degree$ is $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]
So, angle $WXY$ measures $45\degree$.

options.alt: A circle with center point X and four rays. Ray XV points halfway between 5 and 6 o'clock. Ray XW points to 1 o'clock. Ray XY points halfway between 11 and 12 o'clock. Ray XZ points halfway between 8 and 9 o'clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
The circle is divided into $360$ equal parts. Point $A$ represents the center of the circle.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**Which angle measures $90\degree$?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$ around the center.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

How much of a turn is $90\degree$?

$90\degree$ is a $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]
So, the measure of angle $EAD$ is $90\degree$.

options.alt: A circle with center point A and four rays. Ray AE points halfway between 8 and 9 o'clock. Ray AD points halfway between 11 and 12 o'clock. Ray AC points halfway between 1 and 2 o'clock. Ray AB points halfway between 2 and 3 o'clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
The circle is divided into $360$ equal parts. Point $O$ represents the center of the circle.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**Which angle measures $70\degree$?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$ around the center.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

How much of a turn is $70\degree$?

$70\degree$ is a little less than $90\degree$. So $70\degree$ is a little less than $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]
So, the measure of angle $MON$ is $70\degree$.

options.alt: A circle with center point O and four rays. Ray OL points to 9 o'clock. Ray OM points to slightly before 10 o'clock. Ray ON points to 12 o'clock. Ray OP points to 3 o'clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
The circle is divided into $360$ equal parts. Point $A$ represents the center of the circle.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**Which angle measures $105\degree$?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$ around the center.



[[Ōśā image 1]]

How much of a turn is $105\degree$?

$105\degree$ is a little more than $90\degree$. So $105\degree$ is a little more than $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of a turn.



[[Ōśā image 1]]
So, the measure of angle $BAE$ is $105\degree$.

options.alt: A circle with center point A and four rays. Ray AE points halfway between 11 and 12 o'clock. Ray AD points to 12 o'clock. Ray AC points halfway between 1 and 2 o'clock. Ray AB points to 3 o'clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
The circle is divided into $360$ equal parts. Point $O$ represents the center of the circle.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

**Which angle measures $120\degree$?**

[[Ōśā radio 1]]
hints:
A full turn is $360\degree$ around the center.

[[Ōśā image 1]]

How much of a turn is $120\degree$?

$120\degree$ is a $\dfrac{1}{3}$ of a turn.

[[Ōśā image 1]]
So, the measure of angle $NOP$ is $120\degree$.

options.alt: A circle with center point O and four rays. Ray OL points halfway between 3 and 4 o'clock. Ray OM points to 2 o'clock. Ray ON points to 12 o'clock. Ray OP points to 8 o'clock.
